BlobId.description:
Canonical realm-local blob identifier.

The identifier is content-addressed, but storage and GC semantics remain
realm-scoped.

oneOf.description: Plain text content.
Base64-encoded image content.
Base64-encoded inline bytes used for ingress and live execution.
Durable blob reference used by persisted history/runtime state.
Base64-encoded video content.
Base64-encoded inline bytes used for ingress and live execution.

HandlingMode.description:
Handling mode for ordinary content-bearing work.

`Queue` means outer-loop / next-turn handling and leaves the current run
untouched.
`Steer` means inner-loop handling and requests injection at the earliest
admissible cooperative boundary, while remaining ordinary work rather than
an out-of-band control-plane command.

InputSource.description: Source for an input event posted to an agent.

InputStreamMode.description: Whether this input/peer command should reserve a local interaction stream.

oneOf.description: Do not reserve any stream.
Reserve an interaction stream for the command.

ResponseStatus.description:
Typed status for response interactions.

Mirrors `CommsStatus` from `meerkat-comms` — the comms runtime converts at the boundary.

CommsCommandRequest.description:
Typed wire request for `comms/send`.

Variants are serde-tagged on `kind` and validated structurally at the
deserialization boundary. Required fields per kind are enforced by the
type system; invalid discriminators (`source`, `stream`, `handling_mode`,
`status`) become serde deserialization errors rather than runtime
string-match failures.

Cross-field invariants that cannot be expressed structurally (e.g.
`handling_mode` is forbidden on `Accepted` peer responses) are checked
in [`CommsCommandRequest::into_command`].

oneOf.description: Inject input into the local session.
Send a one-way peer message.
Send a request to a peer.

body.description:
Legacy promotion: if `params` is absent, `body` is wrapped as
`{"body": <body>}` for backwards-compatibility with single-string
requesters. Prefer `params`.

oneOf.description: Send a response to a prior peer request.

CommsCommandRequest.title: CommsCommandRequest

ContractVersion.description:
Semantic version for the Meerkat wire contract.

Start at 0.1.0 — allow breaking changes during Phase 1-3 development.
Bump to 1.0.0 when the SDK Builder ships (Phase 5) and external
consumers exist. Before 1.0.0, minor bumps can be breaking.
ContractVersion.title: ContractVersion

WireInputLifecycleState.description: Public input lifecycle state projection used by RPC surfaces.

WireInputStateHistoryEntry.description: Input transition history entry for RPC-facing snapshots.

McpLiveOpStatus.description: Shared status for live MCP operations.

McpLiveOperation.description: Shared operation kind for live MCP operations.

McpLiveOpResponse.description: Response payload for live MCP operations.
McpLiveOpResponse.title: McpLiveOpResponse

McpLiveOpStatus.description: Shared status for live MCP operations.
McpLiveOpStatus.title: McpLiveOpStatus

McpLiveOperation.description: Shared operation kind for live MCP operations.
McpLiveOperation.title: McpLiveOperation

MobProfileBindingInput.description: Profile binding input: either an inline profile or a realm profile reference.

OutputSchema.description:
Configuration for structured output extraction.

When provided to an agent, the agent will perform an extraction turn after
completing the agentic work, forcing the LLM to output validated JSON that
conforms to the provided schema. The extraction JSON becomes the final
response text (schema-only) in [`RunResult`].

compat.description: Compatibility mode for provider lowering (lossy or strict)

name.description: Optional name for the schema (used by some providers)

schema.description: The JSON schema that the output must conform to

strict.description: Whether to enforce strict schema validation (provider-specific)

SchemaFormat.description: Schema format versions supported by Meerkat.

oneOf.description: Forward-compatibility for unknown block types.

MobDefinitionInput.description:
Public mob definition input for `mob/create`.

This mirrors the public creation contract shape. Runtime-owned lifecycle and
bookkeeping fields such as internal owner/runtime bindings,
`session_cleanup_policy`, `is_implicit`, and internal-only profile tool
bundles are intentionally not part of this schema.
MobDefinitionInput.title: MobDefinitionInput

oneOf.description: Forward-compatibility for unknown block types.

WireHandlingMode.description: Public handling mode for mob member delivery.

WireRenderClass.description: Public render class contract for mob member delivery.

WireRenderMetadata.description: Public render metadata contract for mob member delivery.

WireRenderSalience.description: Public render salience contract for mob member delivery.

MobMemberSendParams.description: Request payload for host-side mob member delivery.
MobMemberSendParams.title: MobMemberSendParams

WireHandlingMode.description: Public handling mode for mob member delivery.

WireMemberRef.description:
Server-resolved opaque handle for a mob member.

Encodes `{mob_id, agent_identity}` as a single base64url-encoded token
that callers treat as opaque. The server resolves the current
`AgentRuntimeId` and fence token against the live mob roster on every
dispatch — clients never reason about `generation` or `fence_token`
directly.

Use [`WireMemberRef::encode`] to produce a token and
[`WireMemberRef::decode`] inside an RPC handler to recover the
`(mob_id, agent_identity)` pair before resolving against the runtime.

MobMemberSendResult.description: Response payload for host-side mob member delivery.

member_ref.description:
Server-resolved opaque handle for subsequent member-targeted calls.
App code routes through `member_ref`; the binding-era
`{identity, generation}` pair carried by `WireAgentRuntimeId` is
retired from app-facing responses per dogma #10.

MobMemberSendResult.title: MobMemberSendResult

WireTrustedPeerSpec.description: Minimal trusted peer spec for public mob wiring surfaces.

MobPeerTarget.description: Target for a mob wire/unwire call.
MobPeerTarget.title: MobPeerTarget

RealtimeAudioChunk.description:
An opaque realtime audio chunk with MIME + format metadata.

Both sender and receiver MUST stamp `sample_rate_hz` and `channels` so the
transport layer can validate against the provider session's negotiated
format instead of silently producing garbled audio when an ESP32 or browser
client ships the wrong rate.
RealtimeAudioChunk.title: RealtimeAudioChunk

RealtimeAudioFormat.description: Descriptor for an expected or actual realtime audio format.

RealtimeInputKind.description: Input modality kind supported by a realtime channel.

RealtimeOutputKind.description: Output modality kind supported by a realtime channel.

RealtimeTurningMode.description: Turning mode for a realtime channel.

RealtimeCapabilities.description: Product-facing realtime capability set for one target/provider combination.

audio_input_format.description:
Audio format the provider session accepts for client audio input.
Clients MUST stamp incoming [`RealtimeAudioChunk`]s with the same
`sample_rate_hz` and `channels`; mismatches are rejected server-side.

audio_output_format.description: Audio format the provider session emits for output audio chunks.

RealtimeCapabilities.title: RealtimeCapabilities

RealtimeAudioFormat.description: Descriptor for an expected or actual realtime audio format.

RealtimeCapabilities.description: Product-facing realtime capability set for one target/provider combination.

audio_input_format.description:
Audio format the provider session accepts for client audio input.
Clients MUST stamp incoming [`RealtimeAudioChunk`]s with the same
`sample_rate_hz` and `channels`; mismatches are rejected server-side.

audio_output_format.description: Audio format the provider session emits for output audio chunks.

RealtimeInputKind.description: Input modality kind supported by a realtime channel.

RealtimeOutputKind.description: Output modality kind supported by a realtime channel.

RealtimeTurningMode.description: Turning mode for a realtime channel.

RealtimeAudioFormat.description: Descriptor for an expected or actual realtime audio format.

RealtimeErrorCode.description:
Typed realtime channel error code. Replaces the prior freeform `String` on
[`RealtimeChannelErrorFrame`]; all paths that mint a channel error pick
exactly one variant so downstream SDKs can match without string folklore.

oneOf.description: Incoming frame could not be parsed.
The first frame on a new socket was not `channel.open`.
The open token did not match any pending reservation.
The open token was valid but its TTL elapsed before use.
The client-requested role does not match the reservation.
The client-requested turning mode does not match the reservation.
The reservation requested a turning mode this host does not support.
The target already has an active primary channel.
The client handshake requested a protocol version this host cannot serve.

An input audio chunk's sample rate or channel count did not match the
format the provider session is negotiated for.

The open token was minted for a different realm than the one the
websocket session currently resolves to. Enforced server-side.
A tool dispatch exceeded its configured budget.
Generic internal runtime error (fallback when no better code fits).
Reconnect budget exhausted; channel is closing.
Realtime target did not parse or resolve.
No realtime binding exists for this channel (driver not ready).
Runtime layer returned an internal error that has no more-specific code.
Runtime driver is not in a state that can accept this operation.
Upstream provider session is closed.
Upstream provider session failed to open or progressed fatally.
Upstream provider session is temporarily unavailable.
Input modality is not supported by the negotiated capabilities.
No pending turn on this channel to commit or interrupt.
Observer channels may not send input or control frames.
`channel.open` is valid only as the first frame on a new socket.
`channel.commit_turn` is only valid for explicit-commit turning mode.
Channel is reconnecting; the caller must wait for a ready state.

W3-H: the mob member this channel was bound to has been retired and
its realtime binding released by the MobMachine
(`MemberRealtimeBindingReleased` effect). Signalled only for
`MobMember` targets; `SessionTarget` channels do not encounter this.

RealtimeErrorDetails.description:
Structured typed context carried on a channel error frame. Each variant
corresponds to a specific [`RealtimeErrorCode`] and lets clients match
the reason without parsing the message string.

code.description: Typed error code. Single source of truth for branching in SDKs.

details.description: Structured context keyed to the code, when additional fields are useful.

message.description: Human-readable message suitable for logs and operator surfaces.

RealtimeAudioChunk.description:
An opaque realtime audio chunk with MIME + format metadata.

Both sender and receiver MUST stamp `sample_rate_hz` and `channels` so the
transport layer can validate against the provider session's negotiated
format instead of silently producing garbled audio when an ESP32 or browser
client ships the wrong rate.

RealtimeChannelState.description: Lifecycle state for a realtime channel.

deadline_at.description:
Wall-clock deadline after which the reconnect cycle will be abandoned
and the channel will close with `ReconnectExhausted`. Derived from the
reconnect policy's `max_total_ms` on top of the cycle's start time. RFC
3339 encoded, matches `next_retry_at`. Present only while the channel
is actively reconnecting.

prosody_hint.description:
Opaque, provider-specific prosody annotation (tone / sentiment /
pitch hints). Additive field. Providers that do not surface
structured prosody leave it `None`; compactors and downstream
tools may use this as a hint only — the authoritative transcript
is still `text`.

oneOf.description:
The assistant output for `item_id` was truncated at
`audio_played_ms` because the user barged in. `truncated_text` is the
transcript prefix the user actually heard, as reported by the
provider; when the provider has not yet supplied a re-projected
transcript the field is omitted (`None`) and downstream projectors
should keep the existing transcript until a later emission fills it in.

RealtimeVideoChunk.description: An opaque realtime video chunk with MIME metadata.

RealtimeAudioChunk.description:
An opaque realtime audio chunk with MIME + format metadata.

Both sender and receiver MUST stamp `sample_rate_hz` and `channels` so the
transport layer can validate against the provider session's negotiated
format instead of silently producing garbled audio when an ESP32 or browser
client ships the wrong rate.

RealtimeTextChunk.description: A text chunk for realtime ingress/egress.

RealtimeVideoChunk.description: An opaque realtime video chunk with MIME metadata.

audio_output_format.description: Audio format the provider session emits for output audio chunks.

deadline_at.description:
Wall-clock deadline after which the reconnect cycle will be abandoned
and the channel will close with `ReconnectExhausted`. Derived from the

RealtimeChannelTarget.description:
Target for a public realtime channel.

Two variants, one for each addressing mode:

- `SessionTarget` — standalone sessions (no mob-member continuity). The
  session id is pinned for the channel's lifetime; when that session
  ends, the channel ends.

- `MobMember` — mob-member continuity (W3-H / dogma #4). Identity is the
  canonical anchor, and the server resolves the current bridge session
  on every tick from the MobMachine's `member_realtime_bindings` map.
  Respawn atomically rotates the bound session via the
  `MemberRealtimeBindingRotated` effect; the channel survives without
  any SDK round-trip. A terminal `MemberRealtimeBindingReleased`
  closes the channel with `RealtimeErrorCode::BindingReleased`.
RealtimeChannelTarget.title: RealtimeChannelTarget

RealtimeAudioChunk.description:
An opaque realtime audio chunk with MIME + format metadata.

Both sender and receiver MUST stamp `sample_rate_hz` and `channels` so the
transport layer can validate against the provider session's negotiated
format instead of silently producing garbled audio when an ESP32 or browser
client ships the wrong rate.

RealtimeBargeInTruncateFrame.description:
Payload for `channel.barge_in_truncate`.

Sent by the client the moment it detects the user starting to speak over
the assistant's audio, to tell the server what prefix was actually heard.
Field names mirror OpenAI Realtime's `conversation.item.truncate` so the
provider adapter can map straight through without re-encoding.

audio_played_ms.description:
Milliseconds of assistant audio the client actually played back
before the user interrupted. Must not exceed the true elapsed duration
of the audio content.

content_index.description: Content index within the item (0 for the primary audio/text stream).

item_id.description:
Assistant item whose output is being truncated. Matches the `item_id`
emitted in the public `RealtimeEvent::AssistantTranscriptTruncated`.

RealtimeChannelRole.description: Opening role for a realtime channel.

RealtimeTextChunk.description: A text chunk for realtime ingress/egress.

RealtimeTurningMode.description: Turning mode for a realtime channel.

RealtimeVideoChunk.description: An opaque realtime video chunk with MIME metadata.

oneOf.description:
Report a client-side barge-in with the playback cursor, so the server
can truncate the canonical transcript to what the user actually heard.

WireInputLifecycleState.description: Public input lifecycle state projection used by RPC surfaces.

WireInputStateHistoryEntry.description: Input transition history entry for RPC-facing snapshots.

WireRealtimeAttachmentStatus.description: Public live attachment status projection used by runtime and mob surfaces.

WireRuntimeState.description: Public runtime state projection used by RPC surfaces.

BlobId.description:
Canonical realm-local blob identifier.

The identifier is content-addressed, but storage and GC semantics remain
realm-scoped.

oneOf.description: Plain text content.
Base64-encoded image content.
Base64-encoded inline bytes used for ingress and live execution.
Durable blob reference used by persisted history/runtime state.
Base64-encoded video content.
Base64-encoded inline bytes used for ingress and live execution.

ContentInput.description:
Input content that can be either a plain text string or multimodal content blocks.

Deserializes from either `"text"` or `[{type: "text", text: "..."}, ...]`.
Provides `From<String>` and `From<&str>` for ergonomic construction.

role_name.description: Role name (profile key) for the helper in the mob roster.

oneOf.description:
Skip materially late pending occurrences instead of catching them up.

The scheduler applies a small internal grace window so normal tick
jitter does not immediately misfire on-time work.

Catch up overdue pending occurrences only while they remain within the
configured lateness window.

OutputSchema.description:
Configuration for structured output extraction.

When provided to an agent, the agent will perform an extraction turn after
completing the agentic work, forcing the LLM to output validated JSON that
conforms to the provided schema. The extraction JSON becomes the final
response text (schema-only) in [`RunResult`].

compat.description: Compatibility mode for provider lowering (lossy or strict)

name.description: Optional name for the schema (used by some providers)

schema.description: The JSON schema that the output must conform to

strict.description: Whether to enforce strict schema validation (provider-specific)

PeerMeta.description:
Supplementary metadata attached to a peer for discovery.

The peer's routing name lives in `TrustedPeer.name` / `comms_name`.
`PeerMeta` carries additional context: what the agent does and
arbitrary key/value labels.

# Serde

Both fields use `skip_serializing_if`, so `PeerMeta::default()`
serializes to `{}`.

description.description: What this agent does (e.g. "Reviews pull requests for style issues").

labels.description:
Arbitrary key/value labels (e.g. `{"lang": "rust", "team": "infra"}`).

`BTreeMap` keeps keys sorted for deterministic serialization.

RenderClass.description:
Standardized rendering class for injected ordinary work.

This metadata is for normalization/injection rendering, not for core
runtime scheduling. It lets the system consistently frame peer messages,
external events, tool-scope notices, and similar inputs when they are
rendered into model-visible context.

RenderMetadata.description: Optional rendering metadata carried alongside ordinary work content.

RenderSalience.description:
Constrained salience metadata for injected ordinary work.

This is intentionally a small closed enum so call sites do not invent
arbitrary free-text urgency language.

ScheduleId.description: Opaque identifier for a persisted schedule.

SchemaFormat.description: Schema format versions supported by Meerkat.

SessionId.description: Unique identifier for a session (UUID v7 for time-ordering)

ToolAccessPolicy.description: Tool access control for delegated branches

oneOf.description: Inherit all tools from parent
Only allow specific tools
Block specific tools

BlobId.description:
Canonical realm-local blob identifier.

The identifier is content-addressed, but storage and GC semantics remain
realm-scoped.

oneOf.description: Plain text content.
Base64-encoded image content.
Base64-encoded inline bytes used for ingress and live execution.
Durable blob reference used by persisted history/runtime state.
Base64-encoded video content.
Base64-encoded inline bytes used for ingress and live execution.

ContentInput.description:
Input content that can be either a plain text string or multimodal content blocks.

Deserializes from either `"text"` or `[{type: "text", text: "..."}, ...]`.
Provides `From<String>` and `From<&str>` for ergonomic construction.

role_name.description: Role name (profile key) for the helper in the mob roster.

oneOf.description:
Skip materially late pending occurrences instead of catching them up.

The scheduler applies a small internal grace window so normal tick
jitter does not immediately misfire on-time work.

Catch up overdue pending occurrences only while they remain within the
configured lateness window.

OccurrenceId.description: Opaque identifier for a persisted occurrence.

OutputSchema.description:
Configuration for structured output extraction.

When provided to an agent, the agent will perform an extraction turn after
completing the agentic work, forcing the LLM to output validated JSON that
conforms to the provided schema. The extraction JSON becomes the final
response text (schema-only) in [`RunResult`].

compat.description: Compatibility mode for provider lowering (lossy or strict)

name.description: Optional name for the schema (used by some providers)

schema.description: The JSON schema that the output must conform to

strict.description: Whether to enforce strict schema validation (provider-specific)

PeerMeta.description:
Supplementary metadata attached to a peer for discovery.

The peer's routing name lives in `TrustedPeer.name` / `comms_name`.
`PeerMeta` carries additional context: what the agent does and
arbitrary key/value labels.

# Serde

Both fields use `skip_serializing_if`, so `PeerMeta::default()`
serializes to `{}`.

description.description: What this agent does (e.g. "Reviews pull requests for style issues").

labels.description:
Arbitrary key/value labels (e.g. `{"lang": "rust", "team": "infra"}`).

`BTreeMap` keeps keys sorted for deterministic serialization.

RenderClass.description:
Standardized rendering class for injected ordinary work.

This metadata is for normalization/injection rendering, not for core
runtime scheduling. It lets the system consistently frame peer messages,
external events, tool-scope notices, and similar inputs when they are
rendered into model-visible context.

RenderMetadata.description: Optional rendering metadata carried alongside ordinary work content.

RenderSalience.description:
Constrained salience metadata for injected ordinary work.

This is intentionally a small closed enum so call sites do not invent
arbitrary free-text urgency language.

ScheduleId.description: Opaque identifier for a persisted schedule.

SchemaFormat.description: Schema format versions supported by Meerkat.

SessionId.description: Unique identifier for a session (UUID v7 for time-ordering)

ToolAccessPolicy.description: Tool access control for delegated branches

oneOf.description: Inherit all tools from parent
Only allow specific tools
Block specific tools

WireProviderMeta.description: Provider continuity metadata for transcript blocks.

WireAssistantBlock.description: Transcript block inside a block-assistant message.
WireAssistantBlock.title: WireAssistantBlock

WireAuthError.description:
Stable wire kind for auth errors. Mirrors `meerkat_core::AuthErrorKind`
on the wire as a normalized string.
WireAuthError.title: WireAuthError

WireAuthProfile.description:
Wire projection of [`meerkat_core::AuthProfile`]. Sensitive credential
material is NOT wire-projected — callers that want to read secret
material have to go through the server-side
`auth.profile.get` / `/auth/profiles/:id` endpoints which return
typed redacted shapes. `source_kind` is a discriminator for the
credential-source variant.
WireAuthProfile.title: WireAuthProfile

WireAuthError.description:
Stable wire kind for auth errors. Mirrors `meerkat_core::AuthErrorKind`
on the wire as a normalized string.

WireAuthStatus.description:
Wire projection of the auth-profile status. Returned from
`auth.status.get` / `GET /auth/status/:id`.

last_error.description: Most recent auth error, if any.

WireAuthStatus.title: WireAuthStatus

provider.description:
Provider as a normalized string: `"openai"` / `"anthropic"` /
`"gemini"` / `"self_hosted"`. Wire types don't carry the strict
`Provider` enum so consumers aren't forced to update their
schema when we add new providers.

oneOf.description: Forward-compatibility for unknown block types.
Forward-compatibility for unknown block types.
